name: Jarama Trails
name_original: El Cardoso de la Sierra y el Molino de La Hiruela por los senderos del Jarama
description: Hike a moderate 7.5-mile trail through the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve, following the Jarama River to the historic Molino de La Hiru
region_intro: Hiking around El Cardoso De La Sierra offers diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra de Ayllón in Guadalajara. The region features prominent mountain peaks like Pico del Lobo (2,273 m) and Pico Santuy, alongside a network of valleys, gorges, and rivers such as the Jarama and Jaramilla. Hikers can explore diverse forest types, including oak, pine, and beech, and encounter geological formations of granite, gneiss, and slate. This varied terrain provides a range of experiences for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around El Cardoso De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Jarama Trails, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete, following the Jarama River through the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puerto de la Quesera – Pico del Lobo loop from Puerto de la Quesera, a difficult 9.4 miles (15.2 km) path offering challenging ascents and panoramic views of the highest peaks. Local hikers also love the Molino de La Hiruela – La Hiruela loop from El Cardoso de la Sierra, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through traditional Spanish village landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Hiking around El Cardoso De La Sierra is defined by rugged mountains, lush forests, and serene river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more demanding routes. The routes in El Cardoso De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore El Cardoso De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: El Cardoso De La Sierra offers a diverse range of trails, from moderate paths through river valleys and forests to more demanding routes with significant elevation gain in the mountains. The terrain includes rugged mountains, lush forests, and serene river valleys, with geological formations of granite, gneiss, and slate. You'll find options suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: While many trails in the region can be challenging, there are options suitable for families. For a more relaxed walk through traditional village landscapes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de La Hiruela – La Hiruela loop from El Cardoso de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.1-mile trail.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in El Cardoso De La Sierra. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, several routes in El Cardoso De La Sierra are circular. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1287410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera – Pico del Lobo loop from Puerto de la Quesera&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de La Hiruela – La Hiruela loop from El Cardoso de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate, scenic walk.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore prominent mountain peaks like Pico del Lobo and Pico Santuy, and discover serene river valleys carved by the Jarama and Jaramilla rivers. Don't miss the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, especially during thaw periods or in spring. The area also features diverse forests, including oak, pine, and beech.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: El Cardoso De La Sierra offers unique experiences throughout the year. Autumn is particularly stunning due to the vibrant colors of the diverse forests, including beech, oak, and pine. Spring brings lush greenery and impressive waterfalls. Summer provides shaded trails, while winter offers challenging, snow-covered routes for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1287410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera – Pico del Lobo loop from Puerto de la Quesera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.4-mile path with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding vistas of the highest peaks. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14885721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pico Santuy – El Cardoso de la Sierra loop from Área Recreativa de Hayedo de Montejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for trailheads in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points, such as those near El Cardoso de la Sierra village or the Área Recreativa de Hayedo de Montejo, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before your hike, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking routes in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: Public transport options to reach trailheads in El Cardoso De La Sierra can be limited. While some villages might have bus connections, direct access to many remote hiking starting points often requires a private vehicle. Planning ahead and checking local bus schedules for the specific area you intend to hike is recommended.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Cardoso De La Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, from rugged mountains to serene river valleys, and the opportunity to explore traditional Spanish village life. The challenging ascents and panoramic views from peaks like Pico del Lobo are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of El Cardoso De La Sierra provides numerous opportunities for stunning panoramic views. The challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1287410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Quesera – Pico del Lobo loop from Puerto de la Quesera&lt;/a&gt; offers exceptional vistas of the highest peaks. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4352966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vultures' Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot for expansive scenery.

Q: What kind of forests can I expect to see?
A: El Cardoso De La Sierra boasts diverse forest types, providing beautiful scenery throughout the year. You'll encounter mixes of oak, pine, beech (notably the Hayedo de Montejo), birch, rowan, poplar, and holly trees. These forests offer shaded trails and are particularly vibrant during autumn.
tips: Spanish municipality in the province of Guadalajara, in the autonomous community of Castilla-La Mancha. It has a population of 49 inhabitants.
Bridge that crosses the river Jarama located at the fork in the roads between La Hiruela and the Molino de La Hiruela
Nice place, with tables to rest and recharge after the river excursion. With a little luck you will have to share the sandwich with some ox.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this hike is around 3 hours and 20 minutes. This is an average and can vary based on your pace, stops for photos, and breaks.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Jarama trails?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain. You'll find well-conditioned paths, especially near the Molino de La Hiruela, but also more challenging sections with rocks and potentially slippery stones. The trail winds through beautiful pine and oak forests, as well as picturesque meadows, often following the Jarama River.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness due to varying terrain, including some ascents, descents, and rocky passages. While not for absolute beginners, those with some hiking experience should find it manageable.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the charming village of El Cardoso de la Sierra, the traditional Molino de La Hiruela (a working flour mill), and the scenic Bridge over the Jarama River in La Hiruela. The trail also offers views of the Jarama Valley and borders the renowned Hayedo de Montejo (Montejo Beech Forest).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike these trails?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly recommended for this trail, as the trees along the route display vibrant colors, enhancing the scenic beauty. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery. While possible in other seasons, be mindful of potentially slippery conditions on rocky sections during wet weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: The area encompassing La Hiruela is part of the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve. While there are no general entrance fees for hiking the Jarama trails themselves, access to the Hayedo de Montejo (Montejo Beech Forest), which borders the trail, requires a permit due to its protected status. Check the official Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve website for current permit requirements if you plan to visit the beech forest specifically.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available in or near the villages of El Cardoso de la Sierra or La Hiruela. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends or during peak seasons, as parking can be limited in these small villages.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Senderos del Jarama, El Barranco de El Jarama, GR-88, Camino de la Hiruela, and the Senda Los Oficios de la Vida, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both El Cardoso de la Sierra and La Hiruela are small villages that offer some options for cafes, pubs, and rural accommodation. It's a good idea to check availability and opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as some sections can be rocky and potentially slippery. Don't forget your camera for the beautiful scenery!

waypoints.name: Bridge over the Jarama River in La Hiruela
